definition: Equities, credit, and rates are telling mutually contradictory stories about fiscal sustainability. Rates market pricing significant fiscal risk (elevated term premium), credit spreads remain tight (limited default concern), and equities near highs (limited earnings risk). The equity-implied vs. actual HY spread divergence of 50-75bp is a concrete expression of this inconsistency.
evidence: Gold residual $400-600/oz above model fair value
5y5y forward consistency requires both r* at 2.0-2.5% and lower ERP
Equity multiples embed disinflationary glide path
Rates curve prices 3-4 cuts by year-end
generalist_02's financial conditions paradox validates from 4/5 historical episodes
All agents acknowledge the inconsistency; resolution direction is the disagreement
open_questions: Is there a systematic pattern in which asset class leads adjustment?
Can different markets rationally price different probability distributions without being wrong?
Which market is most likely to reprice first?
Is the inconsistency stable or does it have a convergence forcing mechanism?
Will the inconsistency resolve through inflation repricing upward or growth repricing downward?
Will credit or equity reprice first?
Does the financial conditions paradox delay resolution by 12-18 months?
Is the breakeven-specific component of the inconsistency weaker than originally claimed?
